title: Bean Curd Soup (Doufu Tang)-Chinese Tofu Soup

ingredients.title: Soup Base
items: 6 cups chicken stock or vegetable stock (high quality, preferably homemade)
1 small piece dried shiitake mushroom (optional, soak in warm water 20 min)

ingredients.title: Main Ingredients
items: 1 block soft or silken tofu (300–400 g), cut into 1-inch cubes
100 g fresh or frozen corn kernels (optional, adds sweetness)
1 small carrot, julienned or thinly sliced

ingredients.title: Optional Protein
items: 50–100 g cooked chicken, shrimp, or pork, shredded

instruction.title: Prepare Stock
steps: In a pot, bring chicken or vegetable stock to a gentle simmer.
Add ginger, garlic, and dried shiitake mushroom. Simmer for 10–15 minutes to infuse flavors.
Remove ginger, garlic, and dried mushroom (or leave for stronger flavor).

instruction.title: Add Vegetables and Tofu
steps: Add sliced fresh mushrooms, carrots, and corn to the stock.
Gently add cubed tofu, being careful not to break it.
Simmer gently for 5–7 minutes, just until vegetables are tender.

instruction.title: Season
steps: Add light soy sauce, sesame oil, and salt to taste.
If using protein, add cooked shrimp, chicken, or pork at this stage to warm through.

instruction.title: Finish & Garnish
steps: Turn off the heat and stir in chopped spring onions.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh coriander.
Optional: drizzle a tiny drop of sesame oil for aroma before serving.

chefTips: Use high-quality stock – it’s the backbone of the soup.
Silken tofu gives a silky, luxurious texture; medium-firm works if you prefer more bite.
Gentle simmering is key – avoid boiling after adding tofu to prevent breaking.
Layer flavors: ginger, garlic, mushrooms, and a hint of soy create depth without overpowering tofu.
For presentation, arrange vegetables and tofu evenly and add herbs just before serving.
